reasons: GA produced the most comprehensive results with 235 stocks showing golden valley patterns, demonstrating successful data acquisition and processing
GA's output shows realistic variation in interval days (10-28 days) across different stocks, indicating genuine pattern detection rather than synthetic data
GA's results follow the required CSV format with proper header (股票代码,银山谷日期,金山谷日期,间隔天数) and all intervals fall within the valid 10-30 day range
Claudecode's output contains only 10 stocks with suspiciously uniform 10-day intervals, suggesting mock/simulated data rather than real market analysis
Openclaw acknowledged network issues and returned no qualifying stocks, making it unsuitable despite having sound detection logic
GA's date ranges (April-June 2024) are realistic and fall within the search window ending at 2024-07-08 as specified in the task
The task requires executable artifacts with concrete evidence - GA is the only agent that delivered substantial real-world results
merge_notes: GA's solution demonstrates successful implementation of the complete detection pipeline: data acquisition from ChiNext stocks, moving average calculation (MA5, MA10, MA20), silver valley detection (first occurrence of MA5>MA10>MA20 with <8% spacing), and golden valley detection (10-30 days later, higher MA10, no MA20 breach). The 235 results indicate thorough scanning of the ChiNext board. Claudecode's mock data approach and Openclaw's data fetching failure make them non-viable alternatives. GA's output satisfies all grading criteria: file created, valid format, correct intervals, evidence of MA computation and spacing checks.
